request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We need to return JSON with exactly one verdict per candidate_id. Accept if the item is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject if it's legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence. Allowed category_if_not values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other.

Let's review each candidate:

cand_0bf1c0f146b7065c: operational_sequence about control loop enable/disable to prevent windup. Section 5.1.2. Evidence quote: "Unless otherwise indicated, control loops shall be enabled and disabled based on the status of the system being controlled to prevent windup." This is specific operational guidance. Valid.

cand_55a70e148bd1bf42: operational_sequence about control loop initialization to neutral value. Section 5.1.3. Evidence quote supports it. Valid.

cand_ed1dfcd99a9fac19: operational_sequence about neutral control loop condition. Section 5.1.4. Evidence: "A control loop in neutral shall correspond to a condition that applies the minimum control effect..." Valid.

cand_a2fc7e5d7ad7ef3c: operational_sequence about outdoor air temperature sensor validity. Section 5.1.5.1. Evidence: "Outdoor air temperature sensors at air-handler outdoor air intakes shall be considered valid only when..." Valid.

cand_bf0952ec594fef2b: operational_sequence about global outdoor air temperature calculation. Section 5.1.5.2. Evidence supports average and discard highest/lowest if four or more. Valid.

cand_f08695ea0904329c: operational_sequence titled "Proven ON/OFF Definition" with section_id "5.1.6". Evidence quote: "and differential pressure [DP] control , see Section 5.16.4 .)" This seems like a mis-extraction. The quote doesn't match the title. It appears from source_pages [20]. Possibly the extracted evidence is incomplete or wrong. The summary says: "The term 'proven' means that the equipment's DI status point matches the state set by the equipment's DO command point." That might be from elsewhere, but the evidence quote does not support it. This looks like an unsupported extraction. Should be rejected as unsupported.

cand_750afb57679178b3: operational_sequence titled "Software Point and Software Switch Definition" with section_id 5.1.7. Evidence quote: "5.1.7. The term “software point” shall mean an analog variable, and “software switch” shall mean a digital" truncated. It seems incomplete but still indicates definition. However, the question says reject generic definitions without operational use. This is a definition that might be purely definitional, but it's part of the guideline and could be important for understanding operational sequences. It says "not associated with real I/O points. They shall be read/write capable." It might be considered generic definition but with operational implication (read/write). However, the reject criteria says "generic definitions without operational use". This definition is necessary for understanding software points in sequences. But it's still a definition. I think it's borderline. Looking at the guideline, definitions are part of the standard, but the instruction says: "Reject ... generic definitions without operational use". This definition is indeed generic, just defines terms. But it's referenced in many operational sequences. I'll accept it as valid because it's necessary for interpreting other sequences. The evidence is truncated but still supports it. So I'll accept.

cand_7a4ffe1a5d7b1449: operational_sequence about proportional-only loops for limiting loops. Section 5.1.8.1. Evidence: "Use proportional only (P-only) loops for limiting loops (such as zone CO2 control loops, etc.)." Valid.

cand_577bd3e2a5c30653: operational_sequence about derivative term usage restriction. Section 5.1.8.2. Evidence: "Do not use the derivative term on any loops unless field tuning is not possible without it." Valid.

cand_82a571c442102763: parameter_spec about maximum rate of change for control loop output. Section 5.1.9. Evidence: "To avoid abrupt changes in equipment operation, the output of every control loop shall be capable of being limited by a user adjustable maximum rate of change, with a default of 25% per minute." Valid.

cand_b6d0066caf9b4189: parameter_spec about adjustability of setpoints, timers, deadbands, PID gains. Section 5.1.10. Evidence supports. Valid.

cand_c664a01aa6fccc45: commissioning_step about override capability for all points. Section 5.1.11. Evidence supports. Valid.

cand_031cdcfeba03deb7: parameter_spec about alarm levels definition. Section 5.1.12.1. Evidence: "There shall be 4 levels of alarm a. Level 1: Life-safety message..." This is a definition of alarm levels, but it's operational knowledge because it specifies alarm levels used in sequences. Might be considered generic definition? But it's essential for alarm handling sequences. I think it's valid.

cand_e79a3a0d235cd09b: operational_sequence about maintenance mode alarm suppression. Section 5.1.12.2. Evidence supports. Valid.

cand_6afffaa7b402b0ab: parameter_spec about alarm exit hysteresis. Section 5.1.12.3. Evidence quote: "5.1.12.3. Exit Hysteresis" only. The evidence is just the section header, not the full content. The summary says "Each alarm shall have an adjustable time-based hysteresis (default: 5 seconds) and an adjustable percent-of-limit-based hysteresis (default: 0%) to exit the alarm." The evidence quote is insufficient to confirm the details. Possibly the extraction missed the full text. This could be unsupported. Since evidence is only the section title, I'll consider it insufficient. Reject as unsupported.

cand_71d40bdfa4ea1f7d: parameter_spec about alarm latching defaults. Section 5.1.12.4. Evidence quote: "5.1.12.4. Latching . A latching alarm requires acknowledgment from the operators before it can return to" truncated. Again, insufficient evidence. The summary says "Default latching status: Level 1 alarms: latching, Level 2 alarms: latching, Level 3 alarms: nonlatching, Level 4 alarms: nonlatching." The evidence doesn't confirm these defaults. Reject as unsupported.

cand_227d3540a8daffb0: parameter_spec about post-exit suppression period defaults. Section 5.1.12.5. Evidence: "Default suppression periods are as follows: a. Level 1 alarms: 0 minutes b. Level 2 alarms: 5 minutes c. Level 3 alarms: 24 hours d. Level 4 alarms: 7 days". This evidence fully supports. Valid.

cand_e0a9500fc27a48e8: parameter_spec about VFD speed point configuration. Section 5.1.13.1. Evidence: "The speed AO sent to VFDs shall be configured such that 0% speed corresponds to 0 Hz, and 100% speed corresponds to maximum speed configured in the VFD." Valid.

cand_e1c138714edb7005: operational_sequence about VFD minimum speed monitoring and correction. Section 5.1.13.2. Evidence quote: "5.1.13.2. For each piece o f equipment, the minimum speed shall be stored in a single software point; in the" truncated. The summary says it includes reading every 60 minutes, mismatch writing, and Level 4 alarm. The evidence doesn't confirm the details. Reject as unsupported.

cand_0c214e50fdb79fd6: operational_sequence about Trim & Respond logic overview. Section 5.1.14.1. Evidence: "T&R set-point reset logic and zone/system reset requests, where referenced in sequences, shall be implemented as described below." This is a statement that such logic shall be implemented. It's valid.

cand_7aab617841ddb87b: operational_sequence about Trim & Respond request definition and software points. Section 5.1.14.2. Evidence quote: "5.1.14.2. A “request ” is a call to reset a static pressure or temperature setpoint generated by downstream" truncated. The summary includes specific points and accumulator reset and alarm. The evidence doesn't confirm all that. Reject as unsupported.

cand_00db7217c4f267e7: operational_sequence about equipment staging and rotation requirement. Section 5.1.15.1. Evidence: "Parallel equipment shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear." Valid.

cand_241f91d6ca390545: parameter_spec about runtime points definition. Section 5.1.15.2. Evidence quote: "5.1.15.2. Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment:" truncated. The summary mentions Lifetime and Staging Runtime. The evidence is incomplete. Reject as unsupported.

cand_7dc2e14ce1df2cd2: operational_sequence about lead/lag equipment rotation logic. Section 5.1.15.3. Evidence: "Lead/lag equipment: Unless otherwise noted, identical parallel staged equipment (such as CHW pumps and cooling towers) shall be lead/lag alternated when more than one is off or more than one is on so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the lead stage equipment." This evidence fully supports. Valid.

cand_4949717bdc9cf873: operational_sequence about lead/standby equipment rotation (non-continuous). Section 5.1.15.4. Evidence: "Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment." Valid.

cand_8bd742f752455ad3: operational_sequence about lead/standby rotation for continuously running equipment. Section 5.1.15.4.b. Evidence: "If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off." Valid.

cand_f095a16df0b4b0f6: operational_sequence about variable speed fan/pump deceleration rate. Section 5.1.15.4.1. Evidence: "Variable speed fans and pumps shall have a deceleration rate of 1 Hz/second or slower set in BAS logic when disabled to prevent nuisance trips of operating equipment (e.g., chillers)." Valid.

cand_1f21c0f48dbb5a87: operational_sequence about operator override of staging order. Section 5.1.15.5.a. Evidence: "Operators with appropriate access level shall be able to manually command staging order via software points, but not overriding the In-Alarm or Hand-Operation logic in the following subsections. 1. Staging order changes initiated via operator override shall be instituted as part of normal staging events. 2. Staging order shall remain overridden until released by operators." Valid.

cand_a776be59d72f7879: fault_diagnostic_rule about faulted equipment definition and alarm. Section 5.1.15.5.b.1. Evidence: "A faulted equipment is any equipment commanded to run that is either not running or unable to perform its required duty. If an operating equipment has any fault condition described subsequently, a Level 2 alarm shall be generated and a response shall be triggered as defined below." Valid.

cand_876e1a1c23a75485: fault_diagnostic_rule about fan and pump fault condition. Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.i. Evidence: "Status point not matching its on/off point for 3 seconds after a time delay of 15 seconds while the equipment is commanded on." Valid.

cand_f24e41f3fabd941b: fault_diagnostic_rule about chiller fault conditions. Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.ii. Evidence quote: "5.1.15.5.b.1.ii or a chilled water or condenser water pump dedicated to that chiller is faulted per" truncated. The summary lists specific fault conditions. The evidence only mentions a reference to other sections. The summary might be drawing from other parts. The evidence does not support the detailed list. Reject as unsupported.

cand_7bd959472f943f2e: fault_diagnostic_rule about boiler fault conditions. Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.iii. Evidence quote: "5.1.15.5.b.1.iii or a hot water pump dedicated to that boiler is faulted per Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.i ;" truncated. Similar issue. Reject as unsupported.

cand_e9f53d34f69c3962: operational_sequence about economizer disable based on high limit. Section 5.1.17.1. Evidence quote: "5.1.17.1. Economizer shall be disabled whenever the outdoor air conditions exceed the economizer high -" truncated. The summary includes details about setpoint determination. The evidence is incomplete. Reject as unsupported.

cand_a0b2291ced9a5541: operational_sequence about damper/valve position knowledge requirement. Section 5.1.18.1. Evidence: "Knowledge of damper and valve position are required for proper generation of T&R reset requests." Valid.

cand_83103d1cfcf2e778: operational_sequence about SystemOK flag definition. Section 5.1.19.2. Evidence: "For each system as defined in Section 5.1.19.1.d, there shall be a SystemOK flag, which is either TRUE or FALSE." Valid.

cand_7b8031a5f08ea865: operational_sequence about SystemOK TRUE conditions. Section 5.1.19.3. Evidence: "SystemOK shall be TRUE when all of the following are true: a. The system is proven ON. b. The system is achieving its temperature and/or pressure setpoint(s) for at least 5 minutes c. The system is ready and able to serve its load" Valid.

cand_d7afd0c067ce42b6: operational_sequence about SystemOK FALSE conditions. Section 5.1.19.4. Evidence quote: "5.1.19.4. SystemOK shall be FALSE while the system is starting up (i.e., before reaching setpoint ) or when" truncated. The summary mentions inhibition by alarms. The evidence is incomplete. Reject as unsupported.

cand_632b1a72db806f75: operational_sequence about hierarchical alarm suppression logic. Section 5.1.19.5. Evidence: "The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. a. If SystemOK is FALSE for a cooling water system (i.e., chiller, cooling tower, or associated pump), then only high-temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. b. If SystemOK is FALSE for a heating water system (i.e., boiler or associated pump), then only low temperature alarms from the loads shall be suppressed. c. If SystemOK is FALSE for an air-side system (air handler, fan coil, VAV box, etc.), then all alarms from the loads shall be suppressed." Valid.

cand_d962acdd30ec32fa: operational_sequence about cascading hierarchical suppression. Section 5.1.19.6. Evidence: "This hierarchical suppression shall cascade through multiple levels of load-source relationship such that alarms at downstream loads shall also be suppressed." Valid.

cand_0cdcfc093bc41447: operational_sequence about alarms never suppressed by hierarchical logic. Section 5.1.19.7. Evidence: "The following types of alarms will never be suppressed by this logic: a. Life/safety and Level 1 alarms b. Failure-to-start alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded ON, but status point shows equipment to be OFF) c. Failure-to-stop/hand alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded OFF, but status point shows equipment to be ON)" Valid.

cand_5e97d84fdfdfd834: parameter_spec about time-based suppression default delay periods. Section 5.1.20.1. Evidence: "5.1.20.1. Calculate a time -delay period after any change in setpoint based on the difference between the" truncated. The summary provides detailed defaults. Evidence incomplete. Reject as unsupported.

Now, list all with verdicts:
